rationale: Both correctly tie the failure to PR #4834 (matching yenuo26's comment) and offer the same plausible level=1-vs-level=2 test-bug theory, but both overshoot the actual thread, which ends unresolved with Flink-ddd asking for a full CI re-run rather than any confirmed 'already fixed, close' verdict. X hedges its close recommendation by asking the named participants to confirm the CI commit and re-trigger — which actually echoes the real outstanding ask in the thread — whereas Y asserts a specific PR title, merge date, and named regression tests (test_level2_sleep_wake_raises, Repro A/B/C, a companion RuntimeError guard) that appear nowhere in the given evidence and read as fabricated specificity. Y is somewhat more thorough on user guidance, but that's outweighed by its overconfident, less-grounded closure claim.
